purpose: Compose-and-execute commit panel anchored at the bottom of the Git Manager sidebar. Two textareas (subject + optional body) with the Conventional Commits 50/72 rule visualized via live character counters and a soft-limit warning. The commit button's enabled state derives from `subject.trim().length > 0 && (stagedCount > 0 || amend) && !committing`. Wires Cmd/Ctrl+Enter shortcuts (and a globally focused .git-manager-commit-input element from GitManagerView's Ctrl+Enter shortcut). On success calls api.gitCommit(repoPath, message), then refreshes both gitStatus and gitLog and invokes onCommitComplete (used by the parent to refresh upstream views). Includes an Amend checkbox with a dismissable orange warning banner ('Amend rewrites history'), but amend is currently NOT supported by the backend IPC — the handler short-circuits with an explicit error message.
gotchas: Amend is wired in the UI but the IPC handler does not accept --amend — clicking commit while amend is checked surfaces a hard-coded error and aborts. To enable amend, extend gitCommit IPC to accept an options object.

Newlines are explicitly stripped from the subject onChange (`val.replace(/\n/g, '')`) AND Enter without modifier is preventDefaulted in handleSubjectKeyDown — both guards exist because the textarea has rows=2 but is treated as a single-line field.
The full commit message is built as `subject.trim() + '\n\n' + body.trim()` only when body is non-empty — the blank line separator is mandatory git convention.

Cmd/Ctrl+Enter is handled in TWO places: handleSubjectKeyDown (for subject textarea) and handleKeyDown (for body textarea). Plus a third handler in GitManagerView focuses .git-manager-commit-input on a global Ctrl+Enter — keep all three coherent if changing the shortcut.
commitResult is auto-cleared after 4 seconds via a ref-tracked timeout — clearing the timeout on the next showResult call prevents stale messages from clobbering newer ones.
The amend warning's 'I understand' button only sets amendWarningDismissed but does NOT enable commit — commit is still gated on the amend-not-supported error.
patterns: Subject character counter color flips to var(--color-blocked) when subjectLen > 50; body wrap indicator flips when any line exceeds 72.
Commit button shows an inline staged-count badge (rounded pill with stagedCount) when not amending — visual confirmation of what will be committed.
Refresh after commit fires gitStatus + gitLog in parallel via Promise.all — both must complete before onCommitComplete is invoked.
conventions: Inline styles dominate for the result/warning/counter elements rather than CSS classes — keeps everything self-contained but makes themeing harder.
Uses showResult abstraction with type 'success'|'error' that maps to var(--color-completed)|var(--color-failed).
